commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d64ff361f73129b7dd50809fdaee307a9c9b3d36)
commands.review_summary:
This PR moves shell completion, systemd linger, and startup channel maintenance Doctor repairs into the internal normalized health-check runner and adds focused tests for those conversions.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: the normalized runnable check is registered into core checks, always emits an info finding, and Doctor lint defaults to info severity. I did not execute the CLI because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor revision, real CLI proof, and maintainer review of the stacked runner contract; missing proof also makes it unsuitable for an automated repair lane.

target.title: Doctor: structure core maintenance repairs
target.body:
## Summary

This is PR2 in the Doctor repair stack and sits on #83753.

It moves three core maintenance repairs onto the internal normalized runner path while keeping the public plugin health contract unchanged:

- shell completion health now reports findings from the existing completion status helper and applies cached-completion repair through the runner
- systemd linger health now reports the Gateway linger requirement as a structured repairable check while preserving the existing interactive repair behavior
- startup channel maintenance now runs through the structured Doctor repair runner instead of living only as an ad hoc repair contribution

The public plugin-facing contract remains split `detect(ctx, scope?)` plus optional `repair(ctx, findings)`. The `run(ctx)` shape used here is internal to the core runner and core checks, so this PR does not update plugin SDK exports or generated SDK baselines.

## Review Notes

The main review question is whether these three core maintenance repairs are the right first consumers of #83753's internal runner normalization:

- `doctor --lint` stays read-only and findings-oriented
- `doctor --fix` owns mutation
- dry-run and diff preview are runner presentations over returned changes/effects
- presentation notes remain outside findings and continue through existing note surfaces

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `git diff --cached --check`
- `CI=1 corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --config.confirmModulesPurge=false`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/health-check-registry.ts src/flows/health-check-runner-types.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/health-check-registry.ts src/flows/health-check-runner-types.ts`

Notes:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ...` did not start cleanly in this worktree: first it hit Vitest project `maxWorkers` group-order configuration, and a later single-file wrapper invocation timed out without useful output. The same explicit files passed through the installed Vitest entry directly: 6 files / 33 tests.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...` failed before linting the touched files while preparing unrelated WhatsApp boundary artifacts because `baileys` types were missing. Direct oxlint on the touched files passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors.
- `codex review --uncommitted` was attempted from the WSL worktree and timed out after 15 minutes without returning findings.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Core Doctor maintenance repairs for shell completion, systemd linger, and startup channel maintenance now return structured runner data instead of living only in ad hoc command or contribution flow.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04 source checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-85566-doctor-core`, stacked on #83753 head `d139d39e884019ce5de521660ba382a0499a1575`, current PR head `d64ff361f73129b7dd50809fdaee307a9c9b3d36`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Installed dependencies with frozen lockfile, ran core/test typecheck, ran the six focused Vitest files for shell completion, systemd linger, startup channel maintenance, core checks, repair flow, and lint flow, checked formatting, and ran direct oxlint on the touched files.

Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest passed for 6 files / 33 tests; core test-project `tsgo` passed; core production `tsgo` passed; oxfmt passed on all 8 touched files; direct oxlint reported 0 warnings and 0 errors on all 8 touched files; git diff checks passed.

Observed result after fix: The converted core maintenance checks compile against the internal runner contract, preserve the public split health-check registration API, and have focused coverage for detect/repair, repair validation, and startup maintenance runner behavior.

What was not tested: No full `pnpm check`, no full Vitest suite, no remote Crabbox/Testbox run for this PR2 update yet.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
